items.name: Astro
items.description: The framework behind mikemurphy.ai. I use it for fast pages, Markdown content collections, clean routes, and a site that feels easier to maintain than WordPress for this AI-builder hub.
items.recommendation_reason: The engine that keeps this whole site fast and simple to maintain — picked it specifically so writing and shipping content never feels like a chore.

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.

items.name: 1Password
items.description: Password manager that stores every login, API key, and secret I use across devices.
items.recommendation_reason: Autofill and secure sharing that just work everywhere, plus a CLI for pulling secrets straight into scripts.

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.

items.name: Cloudflare Pages
items.description: Free static site hosting with global CDN, built-in CI/CD from Git, and generous free-tier limits.
items.recommendation_reason: I use this to host and deploy this Astro site straight from Git — zero-config builds, fast global CDN, and a free tier that's never let me down

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.

items.name: VS Code
items.description: Free code editor from Microsoft where I write nearly all of the code behind this site and its projects.
items.recommendation_reason: Extensions cover every language and workflow I need, and it integrates cleanly with Git, Claude Code, and remote dev setups.

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.

items.name: Homebrew
items.description: Command-line package manager for macOS I use to install and update nearly every dev tool on my machine.
items.recommendation_reason: One command to install almost anything, and it keeps my whole toolchain updated without hunting for installers.

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.

items.name: GitHub
items.description: Where every project in this stack lives — version control, issue tracking, and the deploy trigger for this site.
items.recommendation_reason: Everything ties back to a repo here, from Astro deploys to backing up Directus schema changes.

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.

items.name: AirTable
items.description: Spreadsheet-database hybrid I use to track content ideas, resource lists, and behind-the-scenes project data.
items.recommendation_reason: Flexible enough to model almost any workflow — views, linked tables, and automations keep scattered project data organized in one place.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: Beehiiv
items.description: Newsletter platform I use to write and send updates to subscribers, with built-in growth and monetization tools.
items.recommendation_reason: Clean editor and solid analytics without the bloat of older email tools — makes shipping a newsletter issue painless.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: BuzzSprout
items.description: Podcast hosting platform I use to publish episodes and distribute them to Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and everywhere else.
items.recommendation_reason: Handles distribution and analytics without extra setup, so I can focus on recording instead of RSS feed plumbing.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: Cloudflare Registrar & DNS
items.description: Domain registration and DNS administration.
items.recommendation_reason: No markup on renewals, and DNS management sits right alongside everything else I already run through Cloudflare.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Directus
items.description: The headless CMS powering all the content behind mikemurphy.ai — this admin panel is where every resource, post, and project gets managed.
items.recommendation_reason: Self-hosted and flexible enough to shape the data model exactly how each project needs it, instead of fighting a rigid CMS schema.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Docker
items.description: Container platform that packages this site's backend services so they run identically on my laptop and on the VPS.
items.recommendation_reason: Keeps every self-hosted service isolated and reproducible — one config file and it spins up the same way on any machine, no dependency hell.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Hostinger VPS
items.description: The VPS that hosts Directus and every self-hosted service behind this site — the actual server this admin panel runs on.
items.recommendation_reason: Solid price-to-performance for a small personal VPS, with enough headroom to run Docker, Directus, and a handful of other services at once.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: ElevenLabs
items.description: AI voice generator I use to turn scripts into natural-sounding narration for videos and audio content.
items.recommendation_reason: The most realistic text-to-speech and voice cloning I've tried — saves hours versus recording narration myself for every project.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: Epidemic Sound
items.description: Royalty-free music and sound effects library I use to score videos and podcasts without copyright headaches.
items.recommendation_reason: Huge catalog with a straightforward license that covers YouTube and podcasts, so I never worry about takedowns.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: Neon
items.description: Serverless Postgres database with instant branching — the backend data store for my app projects.
items.recommendation_reason: Branch a full copy of the database for testing in seconds, and it scales to zero when nothing's running so I'm not paying for idle compute.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Shlink
items.description: Self-hosted URL shortener that runs on my own VPS instead of a third-party link service.
items.recommendation_reason: Full control over my short links and their click analytics, with no rate limits, ads, or vendor lock-in from a SaaS shortener.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Tailscale
items.description: Zero-config mesh VPN that connects my VPS, laptop, and other devices into one private network.
items.recommendation_reason: Makes it trivial to reach self-hosted services securely from anywhere, without opening ports or running a traditional VPN server.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Termius
items.description: Modern SSH Client. Very handy tool to SSH into Hostinger VPS.
items.recommendation_reason: Very convenient and efficient tool to use for SSH. Free version is generous and nice UI.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Traefik
items.description: Reverse proxy that automatically routes traffic to every Docker container on the VPS and handles HTTPS certificates.
items.recommendation_reason: Auto-discovers new containers and issues SSL certs on its own, so I never have to touch a reverse-proxy config by hand.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Uptime Kuma
items.description: Self-hosted uptime monitor that pings every service on the VPS and alerts me the moment something goes down.
items.recommendation_reason: Clean dashboard and easy alert integrations — it's caught outages long before a user ever had to tell me.

category.name: Infrastructure
category.section_description: Servers, short links, and self-hosted pieces that support tutorials and practical AI workflows.

items.name: Hyperframes
items.description: Tool for wrapping screenshots and clips in polished device frames and mockups for marketing and app store assets.
items.recommendation_reason: Turns plain screenshots into professional-looking device mockups in seconds without opening a design tool.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: Claude Code
items.description: Anthropic's command-line coding agent — reads, edits, and runs code across a whole repo instead of just answering questions in a chat window.
items.recommendation_reason: My default pair-programmer for anything code-related — handles multi-file edits, debugging, and repetitive refactors so I can focus on the parts that actually need a human.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: Remotion
items.description: Framework for creating videos programmatically with React — I use it to auto-generate short-form video content from code.
items.recommendation_reason: Turns data or templates into finished videos without touching a traditional editor, which is huge for repeatable content.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: ScreenFlow
items.description: Screen recording and video editing app I use to capture tutorials and turn them into polished walkthroughs.
items.recommendation_reason: Records, edits, and adds callouts in one app — no need to bounce between separate recording and editing tools.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: Gemini
items.description: Google's AI model — I turn to it for large-context tasks and quick research questions alongside Claude.
items.recommendation_reason: Handles huge documents and long context better than most models I've tried, which makes it my go-to for research-heavy tasks.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: LM Studio
items.description: An Agent made for Open Models. Run Large Language Models (LLMs) securely on your own hardware without an internet connection or cloud API costs.
items.recommendation_reason: This is a good privacy-first agent interface that I keep in the AI Toolbox for mobile and local LLM use.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: n8n
items.description: Self-hosted workflow automation tool I use to connect APIs, schedule jobs, and glue together the other tools in this stack.
items.recommendation_reason: Visual workflow builder that's flexible enough to replace a dozen small scripts, and it runs quietly on the VPS in the background.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: Obsidian
items.description: Local-first Markdown notes app where I keep project notes and drafts before they become published content.
items.recommendation_reason: Plain Markdown files I fully own, plus a graph view and plugin ecosystem that make it easy to link ideas together.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: Ollama
items.description: Runs open-source LLMs locally on my own hardware, no API key or internet connection required.
items.recommendation_reason: Great for testing prompts and models offline or privately, without sending anything to a third-party API.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: OpenAI Codex
items.description: OpenAI's CLI-based coding agent — another option I keep in rotation alongside Claude Code for larger refactors and second opinions.
items.recommendation_reason: Handy for cross-checking tricky bugs or getting a second model's take when Claude Code and I are stuck on the same approach.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: OpenCode
items.description: Anthropic's command-line coding agent — reads, edits, and runs code across a whole repo instead of just answering questions in a chat window.
items.recommendation_reason: The open source AI coding agent that I use occasionally as a Claude Code backup and for Remotion projects because I like the interface and flexibility of adding local models.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: RayCast
items.description: Spotlight-style launcher for Mac that replaces a dozen separate menu bar apps with one fast command palette.
items.recommendation_reason: Extensions for everything from clipboard history to quick calculations — saves dozens of small clicks every day.

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.

items.name: Open WebUI
items.description: IRun AI on your own terms. Connect any model, extend with code, and protect what matters without compromise. Your models, your data, your machine, wherever you open it.
items.recommendation_reason: I have it installed on my VPS and accessible on all devices via Tailscale network

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: Perplexity
items.description: AI-powered search engine that answers questions with cited sources instead of a list of links to sort through.
items.recommendation_reason: My first stop for research questions — faster than a traditional search, and it shows its sources so I can verify claims.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: WisprFlow
items.description: Voice dictation tool that turns speech into clean, formatted text anywhere on my Mac.
items.recommendation_reason: Way faster than typing for drafting notes or messages, and it's smart enough to clean up filler words on its own.

category.name: AI Workbench
category.section_description: The AI coding, notes, and automation tools that stay open while I build and teach.

items.name: Typora
items.description: A simple yet powerful Markdown Reader. Was my daily driver for writing Markdown until I switched to Obsidian, but I still use it frequently.
items.recommendation_reason: One of those must-have tools that I find myself opening daily for writing and formatting Markdown or just using as a scratchpad.

category.name: Content Stack
category.section_description: Publishing, audio, and newsletter tools that help turn experiments into useful resources.

items.name: Zed
items.description: A minimal code editor crafted for speed and collaboration with humans and AI.
items.recommendation_reason: It is my go-to whenever I open up .txt files, code snippets, JSON, etc.

category.name: Builder Stack
category.section_description: The visible and invisible pieces used to build, ship, and maintain this Astro site.
